name: bug-realtime-audit
description: Read-only audit of the realtime call path (2026-07-14), full findings in .overstory/specs/bug-realtime.md. Top confirmed bugs: (1) barge-in dead zone - conversation.ts speech-start only clears when botSpeaking/ttsWorking/frameQueue set, so caller speech during LLM think time is talked over; (2) drain() wall-clock pacing never re-anchors after underrun, so post-filler reply audio bursts (reintroduces choppiness 77992b9 fixed); (3) 20s LLM timeout AbortError treated as barge-in leaves responseComplete=false -> drain polls forever, partial reply never recorded to messages.

name: abort-vs-timeout-discrimination
description: Cancellation discipline for the call path: external cancels (barge-in/call-end) bump speechGen or set closed BEFORE calling abort(), so respond() treats 'AbortError && (gen changed || closed)' as the only skip-bookkeeping path. Internal stream watchdogs surface as TimeoutError (idle watchdog reset per chunk, never a whole-stream cap) and fall through to close the turn: flush tail, record spoken text, set responseComplete so drain() emits response_done. Conflating the two left botSpeaking stuck and dropped spoken text from history (audit F3).

name: in-flight-reply-invariants
description: Two invariants for the paced-audio pipeline: (1) barge-in must key off 'reply in flight' (responseComplete false, ttsStreamPending>0) not just audible playback flags, or the caller's follow-up during LLM think time lets a stale reply play over them (audit F1); bargeIn() must also restore responseComplete=true because the aborted respond() early-returns without touching superseded state. (2) A wall-clock paced sender (drain) must re-anchor startWall = now - sent*FRAME_MS on queue underrun, else the gap accumulates send budget that bursts out when audio arrives (audit F2). Every async producer (REST TTS worker, stream callbacks) re-checks its captured gen against speechGen before mutating shared state or stamping marks.
